title: 12.-Revise SEIU-MC MOU Section 17.13 Special Class Commercial Driver’s License Premium to Add 5% for Out-of-Class Lists

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution revising the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) Local 1021, Maintenance
and Clerical (“Union”) Memorandum of Understanding Section 17.13 Special Class Commercial Driver’s
License Premium to apply 5% differential to employees who maintain a Class A driver’s license and are
approved to serve on out-of-class lists for Tractor Trailer Driver and Construction Equipment Operator.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The revision would add 2% pay to 16 current drivers across both out-of-class lists, for an approximate
amount of $31,482 annually at current base pay rates, funded mainly from Enterprise Funds.
sections.current_situation:
Employees who qualify and have approval to serve in an out-of-class capacity are needed operationally
as a reserve crew to cover illnesses, leaves, and other absences of employees in the career
classifications. The proposed wording in the MOU seeks to clarify how those who serve on the out-of-
class list will receive the differential. The City’s recommendation is that the 5% differential should also be
applied to those who qualify and have approval to serve on the out-of-class list, with the rationale that
reserve crews must also maintain Class A licenses in the same manner as those in the career
classification.
sections.background:
In previous agreements with the Union, employees required to possess and maintain a valid California
Class B driver’s license as a condition of employment received a 3% differential. During the most recent
round of labor negotiations with the Union in 2024, the City agreed to pay an extra 2% differential (for a
total of 5%) to employees required to possess and maintain a Class A commercial driver’s license and
assigned to drive a tractor-trailer vehicle or serving in the classification of Construction Equipment
Operator.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental impacts or opportunities associated with the subject of the report.
sections.rationale:
Employees who qualify and have approval to serve in an out-of-class capacity are needed operationally
to cover illnesses, leaves, and other absences of employees in the career classification and must also
maintain Class A licenses in the same manner as those in the career classification.

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
REVISE SEIU-MC MOU SECTION 17.13 SPECIAL CLASS COMMERCIAL DRIVER’S LICENSE
PREMIUM TO APPLY 5% DIFFERENTIAL FOR CLASS A DRIVERS ON OUT-OF-CLASS LISTS FOR
TRACTOR TRAILER DRIVER AND CONSTRUCTION EQUIPMENT OPERATOR
WHEREAS, employees required to possess and maintain a valid California Class B driver’s license as a
condition of employment received a 3% differential; and
WHEREAS, during the most recent round of labor negotiations with the Union in 2024, the City agreed to
pay an extra 2% differential (for a total of 5%) to employees required to possess and maintain a Class A
commercial driver’s license and assigned to drive a tractor-trailer vehicle or serving in the classification of
Construction Equipment Operator; and
WHEREAS, employees who qualify and have approval to serve in an out-of-class capacity are needed
operationally as a reserve crew to cover illnesses, leaves, and other absences of employees in the career
classification; and
WHEREAS, employees on the out-of-class lists are required to maintain a Class A license in the same
manner as those in the career classification; and
WHEREAS, the proposed wording seeks to clarify how those who serve on the out-of-class list will
receive the differential; and
WHEREAS, the City’s recommendation is that the 5% differential should also be applied to those who
qualify and are approved to serve on the out-of-class lists for Tractor Trailer Driver or Construction
Equipment Operator, with that rationale that reserve crews are needed for coverage at all times, and
those individuals are required to maintain a Class A license in the same manner as those in the career
classification.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that employees with
approval to serve on the out-of-class list for Tractor Trailer Driver or Construction Equipment Operator,
who maintain a Class A license, shall receive a 5% differential retroac
